input.instructions: Determine the smallest prime number p such that there exists an integer n satisfying the congruence n^4 + 1 ≡ 0 (mod p^2). After identifying this prime p, find the smallest integer m for which m^4 + 1 ≡ 0 (mod p^2) holds true. Provide your answer in the exact format: FINAL_ANSWER: <p, m> JUSTIFICATION: <brief explanation of how p and m were found>.

Question:
Determine the smallest prime number p such that there exists an integer n satisfying the congruence n^4 + 1 ≡ 0 (mod p^2). After identifying this prime p, find the smallest integer m for which m^4 + 1 ≡ 0 (mod p^2) holds true. Provide your answer in the exact format: FINAL_ANSWER: <p, m> JUSTIFICATION: <brief explanation of how p and m were found>.
